desc: Striking black and white longhorn beetle. Found on alder, willow, ash. One of NA's most beautiful beetles.
Massive brown longhorn beetle to 60mm. Larvae bore into tree roots. Attracted to lights.
Brilliant metallic blue leaf beetle on milkweed. Sequesters cardenolides from host plant.
Large click beetle with prominent false eyespots. Larvae predatory in rotting wood.
Golden-yellow scarab. Nocturnal; attracted to lights. Larvae feed on roots.
Black beetle with red-orange spots. Found on shelf fungi and bracket mushrooms.
Largest beetle in western NA. Males with prominent horn. Found in mountain oak woodland.
Metallic green blister beetle. Produces cantharidin. Swarms on desert flowers after rain.
Metallic violet-blue blister beetle. Hypermetamorphic — larvae parasitize ground-nesting bees.
Yellow with three black stripes. Pest of cucurbits. Vector of bacterial wilt.
Tiny ant-like flower beetle. Found in coastal sage scrub on buckwheat blooms.
Green with 12 black spots. Larva is the western corn rootworm. Major agricultural pest.
Tiny bark beetle found under bark of dead oaks and conifers. Predator of wood-boring larvae.
Desert darkling beetle coated in waxy blue-white bloom. Extremely tough exoskeleton.
Bark beetle affecting ponderosa pine. Major forest pest in mountain areas.
Large brown longhorn of conifer forests. Larvae bore in dead pine and fir.
Introduced snout beetle. Flightless pest of ornamental plants. Nocturnal.
Bioluminescent beetle — larviform females glow green. Males winged, attracted to lights.
Inflated blue-black blister beetle of Mojave margins. Walks slowly with abdomen raised.
Bright red flattened beetle under bark of dead trees. Larvae freeze-tolerant.
Elongate weevil with snout longer than body. Breeds in hardwood logs. Males fight over females.
Ancient relict beetle of cold mountain streams. Poor swimmer — clings to rocks in rapids.
Black and yellow leaf beetle on willows and poplars. Larvae and adults skeletonize leaves.
Flightless black longhorn on prickly pear and cholla. Larvae bore into cactus pads.
Green with cream stripes. Found on cucurbit flowers in summer gardens.
Red and black predatory beetle. Hunts bark beetles on pines — important biocontrol agent.
